rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LIGHTFOOT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 4, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFF ROBERT LIGHTFOOT'S Motion To Tax Costs. Denied. The claimed costs were reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation. The Court did not consider the arguments that were raised for the first time in the reply brief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. DBA GREAT VS. THE EIGER, LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Monetary Sanctions For Failure To Attend Deposition And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 4, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT FLO-SHOW, LLC's Motion For Monetary Sanctions For Failure To Attend Deposition. (Part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: MOTION IS DENIED. IT APPEARS FROM THE PAPERS THAT DEFENDANT FLO-SHOW L.L.C. WAS AWARE PRIOR TO THE DATE OF THE NOTICED DEPOSITION THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT INTEND TO PROCEED WITH THE DEPOSITION. NO SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, PRESENT IT TO COUNSEL FOR FLO-SHOW FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND SUBMIT IT TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING SCHEDULED FOR SEPT. 4, 2012 AT 9:00 AM. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. ANSHEN + ALLEN, ARCHITECTS, INC. et al*****TRANSFERRED TO ALAMEDA COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion To Compel (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 4, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT STANTEC ARCHITECTURE INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions. (Part 2 of 3.) HOWEVER, IN THE MAJORITY OF THE INTERROGATORIES WHERE THE COURT IS REQUIRING ADDITIONAL RESPONSES BY CCSF, IT IS OF THE OPINION THAT WHAT IS BEING REQUESTED IS FACTUAL INFORMATION TO WHICH THE DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO ENABLE IT TO PREPARE ITS DEFENSE. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: NO. 1. CCSF WILL SUPPLEMENT ITS RESPONSE BY IDENTIFYING WITH SPECIFICITY THE 95 ASIs & OVER 80 RFIs REFERRED TO OF PG. 6, LN. 11-13 ON ITS INITIAL RESPONSE. CCSF WILL SUPPLEMENT ITS RESPONSE BY IDENTIFYING WITH SPECIFICITY THE OVER 100 CHANGE ORDERS REFERRED TO ON PG. 7, LN. 8 OF ITS INITIAL RESPONSE. NO. 2. CCSF WILL SUPPLEMENT ITS RESPONSE BY IDENTIFYING WITH SPECIFICITY THE 95 ASIs & OVER 80 RFIs REFERRED TO ON PG. 11, LN. 4-6 OF ITS INITIAL RESPONSE. NO. 3,4,5,22. DENIED. THE REFERENCE BACK TO EITHER NO.1 OR 2 IS SUFFICIENT TO PERMIT DEFENDANT TO PREPARE ITS DEFENSE ONCE THE ADDITIONAL RESPONSES ARE RECEIVED. NO. 6,17,19,20,21. DENIED. THE RESPONSES ARE SUFFICIENT TO PERMIT DEFENDANT TO PREPARES ITS DEFENSE TO THE ALLEGATIONS. NO. 25&50. DENIED ON THE GROUNDS THAT THESE CALL FOR EXPERT OPINIONS & OR ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT. NO. 26,27,28,42,43,44,45,47,48,49&51. DENIED. BY REFERENCE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NO 1, 2 & 94 DEFENDANT HAS SUFFICIENT INFORMATION TO PERMIT IT TO PREPARE ITS DEFENSE. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. ANSHEN + ALLEN, ARCHITECTS, INC. et al*****TRANSFERRED TO ALAMEDA COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion To Compel (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 4, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT STANTEC ARCHITECTURE INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions. (Part 3 of 3.) NO. 79 & 94. DENIED. THE RESPONSES ARE SUFFICIENTLY DETAILED TO PROVIDE DEFENDANT WITH SUFFICIENT INFORMATION TO PERMIT IT TO PREPARE ITS DEFENSE. THE COURT IS NOT INCLINED TO IMPOSE SANCTIONS ON EITHER PARTY. COUNSEL FOR CCSF SHOULD PREPARE AN ORDER FOR SUBMISSION TO COUNSEL FOR STANTEC FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM & SUBMIT TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON 9/4/12. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. ANSHEN + ALLEN, ARCHITECTS, INC. et al*****TRANSFERRED TO ALAMEDA COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set No One And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 4, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT STANTEC ARCHITECTURE INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions. (Part 1 of 3.) Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART & DENIED IN PART. IN ADDRESSING THE SPECIFIC INTERROGATORIES TO WHICH STANTEC SEEKS FURTHER ANSWERS THE COURT HAS CONSIDERED THE POSITION ASSERTED BY CCSF THAT THE REQUESTED INFORMATION CALLS FOR PREMATURE DISCLOSE OF EXPERT WITNESSES TESTIMONY OR COUNSEL'S WORK PRODUCT. AS NOTED IN THE RULINGS LISTED BELOW, THE COURT HAS ACCEPTED THAT ARGUMENT WHERE THE INTERROGATORY ON ITS FACE APPEARED TO CALL FOR SUCH INFORMATION; =(302/JPT)
